content.content:
141	template <const int BrOrBc, const int kTileSize, const int kHeadDim,
142	          const int kMmaAtomK, const int kNumThreads, const int kPad,
143	          typename kDataType = __half>
144	__device__ __forceinline__ void cp_async_qkv_g2s(
145	    uint32_t smem_base_ptr,     // QKV smem base ptr
146	    const kDataType* gmem_ptr,  // QKV gmem ptr
147	    const int gmem_offset,      // QKV gmem_offset
148	    const int n_tile_id,  // seqlen offset, Q_tile_id * Br, tile_K_seqlen * Bc
149	    const int d_tile_id,  // headdim offset, tile_K_d * kMmaAtomK, tile_V_d *
150	                          // kMmaAtomN * 2
151	    const int stage,      // stage * QKV tile_size
152	    const int seqlen_bound = INT_MAX  // bound on the seqlen axis; rows with
153	                                      // global idx >= seqlen_bound are
154	                                      // zero-filled via cp.async src-size=0
155	) {
156	  // QK: tile_K_d < (kHeadDim / kMmaAtomK)
157	  //  V: tile_V_d < (kHeadDim / kMmaAtomN * 2)
158	  if (d_tile_id >= (kHeadDim / kMmaAtomK)) {
159	    return;
160	  }
161	  const int tid = threadIdx.x;  // within block
162	  constexpr bool kSwizzle = (kPad == 0) ? true : false;
163	  // Mapping QKV tid -> smem, tile size [64/128, 16]
164	  // Br 64, tid / 2, row 0~64
165	  const int load_smem_BrOrBc = (tid / (kNumThreads / BrOrBc));
166	  // (tid % 2) * 8, 0,8,...
167	  const int load_smem_d =
168	      (tid % (kNumThreads / BrOrBc)) * (kMmaAtomK / (kNumThreads / BrOrBc));
169	  // Mapping QKV tid -> gmem, tile size [64/128, 16], row offset by
170	  // n_tile_id(seqlen), col offset by d_tile_id(Headdim).
171	  const int load_gmem_BrOrBc = (n_tile_id * BrOrBc) + load_smem_BrOrBc;
172	  const int load_gmem_d = (d_tile_id * kMmaAtomK) + load_smem_d;  // 0,8
173	  // Offset by QKV global gmem_offset.
174	  const int load_gmem_addr =
175	      (gmem_offset + load_gmem_BrOrBc * kHeadDim + load_gmem_d);
176	  // Seqlen boundary predicate: rows beyond QKV_seqlen must be zero-filled.
177	  const bool row_valid = (load_gmem_BrOrBc < seqlen_bound);
178	
179	// cp async & apply swizzle or padding.
180	#pragma unroll
181	  for (int i = 0; i < (kMmaAtomK / (kNumThreads / BrOrBc)); i += 8) {
182	    const uint32_t load_smem_ptr =
183	        (smem_base_ptr +
184	         (stage * kTileSize + load_smem_BrOrBc * (kMmaAtomK + kPad) +
185	          (kSwizzle
186	               ? swizzle::permuted<kMmaAtomK>(load_smem_BrOrBc, load_smem_d + i)
187	               : load_smem_d + i)) *
188	             sizeof(kDataType));
189	    cp_async::cp_async_zfill<16>(load_smem_ptr, &(gmem_ptr[load_gmem_addr + i]),
190	                                 row_valid);
191	  }
192	  // cp_async::commit_group();
193	}
194	
195	// Sync s2r (shared -> register) load of one MMA fragment of Q, K, or V
196	// using ``ldmatrix.sync``. Selects ``ldmatrix.m8n8.x2``, ``x2.trans``
197	// (for V, emits col-major fragments consumed by P@V), or ``x4`` (for Q)
198	// based on ``kTrans`` / ``kNumRegs``. SMEM addressing mirrors the g2s
199	// layout (swizzle when kPad == 0, else padded).
200	//
201	// ``kSmemColStride`` (optional, default 0 = "natural" per-branch stride):
202	// overrides the row stride and the swizzle width used by the K/V
203	// branches. Set to a value > kMmaAtomK (16, 32, 64) to read from a
204	// wider "super-tile" smem layout where one TMA box holds
205	// ``kSmemColStride / kMmaAtomK`` consumed sub-tiles in a single wider
206	// row. ``subtile_col_offset`` selects which sub-tile within that row
207	// the current ldmatrix should target (0 / 16 / 32 / 48 fp16 cols).
208	// The Q branch (kNumRegs == 4) ignores both knobs.
209	template <const int kTrans, const int kNumRegs, const int kTileSize,
210	          const int kMmaAtomM, const int kMmaAtomN, const int kMmaAtomK,
211	          const int kPad, typename kDataType = __half,
212	          const int kSmemColStride = 0>
213	__device__ __forceinline__ void sync_fetch_qkv_frags_s2r(
214	    uint32_t smem_base_ptr,  // QKV smem base ptr
215	    uint32_t* R,             // Register ptr, R_QKV
216	    const int mma_tile_id,   // Q warp_QP 0~num MMAs, KV warp_KV 0
217	    const int warp_tile_id,  // Q 0, KV 0~kValTileSeqLenK
218	    const int n_tile_id,     // seqlen QK 0, V tile_V_Bc
219	    const int stage,         // ring index into smem
220	    const int subtile_col_offset =
221	        0)  // sub-tile col base inside a wide TMA box
222	{
223	  const int lane_id = threadIdx.x % WARP_SIZE;  // 0~31
224	  constexpr bool kSwizzle = (kPad == 0) ? true : false;
225	  if constexpr (kTrans) {
226	    // load V m8n8x2 via ldmatrix.x2.trans
227	    static_assert(kNumRegs == 2);
228	    // mma_tile_id = warp_KV == 0, warp_tile_id = (j % 2), n_tile_id = tile_V_Bc
229	    // warp_smem_V_d  = warp_KV * (kMmaAtomN * kValTileHeadDimV) + (j % 2) *
230	    // kMmaAtomN;
231	    constexpr int kVRowStride =
232	        (kSmemColStride > 0) ? kSmemColStride : (kMmaAtomN * 2);
233	    constexpr int kVSwizzleW =
234	        (kSmemColStride > 0) ? kSmemColStride : (kMmaAtomN * 2);
235	    const int warp_smem_d = warp_tile_id * kMmaAtomN;
236	    const int lane_smem_Bc = n_tile_id * kMmaAtomK + lane_id % 16;
237	    const int lane_smem_d =
238	        subtile_col_offset + warp_smem_d;  // 0,8 (+ box subtile base)
239	    const uint32_t lane_smem_ptr =
240	        (smem_base_ptr +
241	         (stage * kTileSize + lane_smem_Bc * (kVRowStride + kPad) +
242	          (kSwizzle ? swizzle::permuted<kVSwizzleW>(lane_smem_Bc, lane_smem_d)
243	                    : lane_smem_d)) *
244	             sizeof(kDataType));
245	    mma::ldmatrix_m8n8x2_trans(&R[0], &R[1], lane_smem_ptr);
246	  } else {
247	    static_assert(kNumRegs == 2 || kNumRegs == 4);
248	    if constexpr (kNumRegs == 4) {
249	      // load Q m8n8x4 via ldmatrix.x4
250	      // mma_tile_id = warp_QP, kValTileSeqLenQ=1
251	      // warp_smem_Q_Br = warp_QP * (kMmaAtomM * kValTileSeqLenQ) + 0 *
252	      // kMmaAtomM
253	      const int warp_smem_Br = mma_tile_id * (kMmaAtomM);
254	      const int lane_smem_Br = warp_smem_Br + lane_id % 16;  // 0~15
255	      const int lane_smem_d = (lane_id / 16) * 8;            // 0,8
256	      const uint32_t lane_smem_ptr =
257	          (smem_base_ptr +
258	           (stage * kTileSize + lane_smem_Br * (kMmaAtomK + kPad) +
259	            (kSwizzle ? swizzle::permuted<kMmaAtomK>(lane_smem_Br, lane_smem_d)
260	                      : lane_smem_d)) *
261	               sizeof(kDataType));
262	      mma::ldmatrix_m8n8x4(&R[0], &R[1], &R[2], &R[3], lane_smem_ptr);
263	    } else {
264	      // load K m8n8x2 via ldmatrix.x2
265	      // mma_tile_id = warp_KV == 0, warp_tile_id = j
266	      // warp_smem_Bc = warp_KV * (kMmaAtomN * kValTileSeqLenK) + j * kMmaAtomN;
267	      constexpr int kKRowStride =
268	          (kSmemColStride > 0) ? kSmemColStride : kMmaAtomK;
269	      constexpr int kKSwizzleW =
270	          (kSmemColStride > 0) ? kSmemColStride : kMmaAtomK;
271	      const int warp_smem_Bc = warp_tile_id * kMmaAtomN;
272	      const int lane_smem_Bc = warp_smem_Bc + lane_id % 8;  // 0~7
273	      const int lane_smem_d =
274	          subtile_col_offset + ((lane_id / 8) % 2) * 8;  // 0,8 (+ box subtile)
275	      const uint32_t lane_smem_ptr =
276	          (smem_base_ptr +
277	           (stage * kTileSize + lane_smem_Bc * (kKRowStride + kPad) +
278	            (kSwizzle ? swizzle::permuted<kKSwizzleW>(lane_smem_Bc, lane_smem_d)
279	                      : lane_smem_d)) *
280	               sizeof(kDataType));
281	      mma::ldmatrix_m8n8x2(&R[0], &R[1], lane_smem_ptr);
282	    }
283	  }
284	}

286	// Apply a -inf mask to R_S fragments whose local KV column falls beyond the
287	// valid KV length of the current tile. Used on the last KV tile when
288	// ``QKV_seqlen % Bc != 0`` so that online safe-softmax treats padding
289	// columns as logits of -inf (exp(-inf)=0), making them invisible to both
290	// row_max and row_sum without changing the main-path performance.
291	//
292	// The fragment-to-column mapping follows the ``m16n8k16`` C-layout:
293	// for j in [0, kValTileSeqLenK), R_S[j] covers local columns
294	// ``j*8 + (lane_id%4)*2 + {0, 1}`` (and two rows offset by 0 / 8).
295	// With ``kMmaTileSeqLenK == 1`` the warp_KV base is always 0.

297	          typename kDataType = __half>

1222	// ============================================================================
1223	// ffpa_attn_persistent_d_fwd_template
1224	// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
1225	// Split-Q (FlashAttention-2) prefill attention kernel tuned for *small* head
1226	// dimensions (D <= 128, up to 256). Unlike the "large-d" variant, this kernel
1227	// persists the full Q / K / V tile across head-dim within one KV step (no
1228	// inner tile_K_d loop), matching the classical FA-2 schedule. It therefore
1229	// uses more SMEM but fewer synchronizations, giving better throughput when D
1230	// is small.
1231	//
1232	// Supports cross-attention, GQA/MQA, and runtime causal attention with the
1233	// same semantics as the large-d template; see that header for the full
1234	// contract.
1235	//
1236	// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
1237	// Template parameters (compile-time configuration)
1238	// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
1239	//   kDataType            Activation dtype: __half or __nv_bfloat16.
1240	//   kHeadDim             Head dimension D; must satisfy D <= 256
1241	//   (static_assert). kMmaAtomM/N/K        MMA atom shape for m16n8k16 (fixed to
1242	//   16/8/16). kMmaTileSeqLenQ/K/P  Warp-level MMA tiling along Q rows / K cols
1243	//   / P rows. kMmaTileHeadDimV     Warp-level MMA tiling along V cols
1244	//   (head-dim). kValTileSeqLenQ/K/P  Value-level multipliers forming Br, Bc as
1245	//   in the
1246	//                        large-d template.
1247	//   kValTileHeadDimV     Value tiling over head-dim for P@V.
1248	//   kMmaAccFloat32QK/PV  0 -> fp16 MMA accumulator, 1 -> fp32 accumulator
1249	//                        (bf16 requires 1).
1250	//   kOStorageAccFloat32  0/1, O accumulator storage dtype selector.
1251	//   kPrefetchQK/PV       0/1, enable async prefetch of next QK / PV tiles.
1252	//   kShareSmemQKV        0/1, share QK SMEM region when kPersistQs2r is on.
1253	//   kPersistQs2r         0/1, persist Q registers across all KV tiles.
1254	//   kPersistVs2r         0/1, persist the full V tile in registers across the
1255	//                        P@V inner loop (only viable for small D).
1256	//   kRegPipeKV           0/1, register ping-pong between ldmatrix and mma.
1257	//                        Mutually exclusive with kPersistVs2r.
1258	//   kStageQK/kStagePV    Must both be 1 for this template (static_assert).
1259	//   kPadQ/kPadK/kPadV    0 -> SMEM swizzle, >0 -> padded SMEM layout.
1260	//
1261	// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
1262	// Runtime parameters
1263	// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
1264	//   Q   in : fp16/bf16 tensor, shape [Nb, Nh, Nq,  D].
1265	//   K   in : fp16/bf16 tensor, shape [Nb, Nh_kv, Nkv, D].
1266	//   V   in : fp16/bf16 tensor, shape [Nb, Nh_kv, Nkv, D].
1267	//   O   out: fp16/bf16 tensor, shape [Nb, Nh, Nq,  D]; written in place.
1268	//   Nb     : Batch size; combined with Nh to fan out across gridDim.y.
1269	//   Nq     : Query sequence length; drives gridDim.x = div_ceil(Nq, Br) and
1270	//            the tail-row store predicate.
1271	//   Nkv    : Key/Value sequence length (Nk == Nv); drives the KV loop bound
1272	//            Tc = div_ceil(Nkv, Bc) and the tail-tile softmax mask.
1273	//   Nh     : Number of Q attention heads (Nh_q); combined with blockIdx.y
1274	//            to recover (batch, head) indices.
1275	//   Nh_kv  : Number of K/V heads (GQA/MQA). Must divide Nh.
1276	//   scale  : Softmax pre-scale, typically 1 / sqrt(D).
1277	//   Tc     : Precomputed KV tile count = div_ceil(Nkv, Bc).
1278	//   causal : 0/1 runtime flag; identical semantics to the large-d kernel
1279	//            (queries aligned to the KV tail, mask k > r + (Nkv - Nq)).
1280	//
1281	// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
1282	// Grid / block layout (identical shape to the large-d template)
1283	// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
1284	//   Block: (WARP_SIZE * kMmaTileSeqLenQ * kMmaTileSeqLenK, 1, 1).

I now have everything I need. I have a comprehensive understanding of:

1. **mma.cuh** — PTX mma instruction wrappers (m16n8k16 with f16/f32/bf16 accumulators, MMAMode kInplaceUpdate/kAutoZeroFill), ldmatrix instructions (x4, x2, x2.trans).

2. **cp_async.cuh** — cp.async commit/wait groups, cp.async.cg/ca with zfill (for OOB), ldg_sync_128b/stg_sync_128b (128-bit load/store).

3. **tma.cuh** — TMA (Tensor Memory Accelerator) descriptors via cuTensorMapEncodeTiled, mbarrier-based async completion (init_barrier, wait_barrier_parity, load_2d with barrier_arrive_tx), issue_load_2d_to_dst_swizzled / scratch+repack variants. Experimental, gated by ENABLE_FFPA_EXPERIMENTAL_TMA, major>=9.

4. **swizzle.cuh** — permuted swizzle function for bank-conflict-free SMEM (SWIZZLE_32B/64B/128B matching CuTe Swizzle<B,4,3>).

5. **prefill.cuh** — helper functions: cp_async_qkv_g2s (g2s with swizzle/pad/zfill), sync_fetch_qkv_frags_s2r (s2r via ldmatrix), sync_online_safe_softmax, sync_rescaling_tiling_o, sync_rescaling_final_o, sync_update_max_expsum, sync_store_o_r2g, sync_store_lse_r2g, sync_apply_causal_mask/kv_mask/attn_bias/dropout.

7. **launch_templates.cuh** — the launcher that computes Br/Bc/stages/pad/swizzle/persist knobs, sets SMEM via cudaFuncSetAttribute, and dispatches small-d vs large-d vs decode.

8. **ffpa_attn_api.cc** — pybind unified entry ffpa_attn_forward dispatching by (dtype, acc).
